hypothesis: MODEL surface only (ovp-llm request/reply/anthropic + fixture): extending ModelRequest/ModelReply with provider-neutral tool definitions, content blocks (text | tool_use), tool_result user blocks, and mapped stop reasons makes a tool-capable conversation representable and replayable WITHOUT any runtime loop change (ask still single-shot at this version). Predicted: all existing text-only cassettes replay byte-identical (request_key unchanged for tool-less requests), and the new protocol passes the full A1a gate matrix against recorded Anthropic tool-use fixtures. Corroboration: the Anthropic tool-use protocol is the industry convergence point for production agent runtimes (local competitive research, not committed).

predicted_delta.operational_reliability: tool-only replies parse as success (today anthropic.rs:90 treats empty text as decode error); unknown stop reasons never masquerade as final
predicted_delta.cost_efficiency: no change at this version (protocol only, no loop)

guardrails.text_only_compat: every existing cassette replays unchanged; request_key for a request WITHOUT tools is byte-identical to pre-change (tools field absent => key input unchanged)
guardrails.request_key_policy: tools enter the request_key as (tool name, protocol version) pairs only — NEVER the full JSON schema — so schema doc tweaks do not invalidate cassettes; schema changes bump model.ask_tool_protocol version instead
guardrails.tool_call_id_determinism: tool_use ids are provider-issued and preserved verbatim through ModelReply -> transcript -> next-request tool_result; the runtime NEVER generates its own ids (replay of a recorded session must reproduce identical follow-up request bodies)
guardrails.tool_only_success: a reply containing only tool_use blocks and no text is a SUCCESS, not a decode error
guardrails.block_order: text/tool_use blocks preserve provider order; multiple tool_use per assistant turn supported
guardrails.tool_result_adjacency_all: ALL tool_use ids from an assistant turn must be answered by tool_result blocks at the FRONT of the next user message — including failed executions (is_error: true); partial responding is a protocol violation the types make unrepresentable or the builder rejects
guardrails.max_tokens_mid_tool: stop_reason == max_tokens while a tool_use block is open/truncated => the tool call is INVALID: never executed, surfaced as a protocol error (retry-with-larger-budget is the caller's decision)
guardrails.stop_reasons: map at minimum ToolUse | EndTurn | MaxTokens | Refusal + Unknown(String); Unknown is never treated as final success
guardrails.no_runtime_change: ask.rs / server ask path untouched at this version — single Candidate, single surface

eval_plan.replay_set: all existing text-only cassettes (byte-identical replay) + new recorded Anthropic tool-use fixtures: tool-only reply; text+2 tool_use (order+ids); tool_result ok + is_error; 3 calls with 1 failed executor (all three results present, one is_error); max_tokens truncating a tool_use; unknown stop reason

rollback: The tools/content-block fields are additive Options on ModelRequest/ModelReply; revert = stop constructing them (no caller passes tools yet). Cassettes recorded without tools stay valid either way.
